response.reasoning: The candidate is a Staff-level engineer (2025-present) with 14 years of professional experience (just below the 15-year minimum) and clear career progression from developer to senior roles. Education meets baseline (BS Computer Science) but lacks the preferred Master's/PhD in AI. Technical breadth is exceptional—full-stack across web, mobile, hardware, and GIS—and current role at re:cinq mentions AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks. However, the resume shows recent entry into AI-adjacent areas (2025 onward) rather than demonstrated depth: no evidence of building AI/ML systems, training models, or shipping AI-powered products. The AI exposure is in developer tooling (MCP code generators) rather than AI strategy or product. Leadership is present through teaching and founding GISCollective, but strategic vision and customer advisory experience are not evident. The role requires 'recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated products or platforms built with AI'—this candidate has neither.
strengths: Director-level IC status (Staff Engineer) with clear progression and demonstrated mentorship through workshops and Refactoring Dojos
Exceptional technical versatility across web, mobile, embedded, GIS, and real-time systems; proven ability to learn new domains and tools quickly
Current exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq; entrepreneurial founding of GISCollective shows ability to drive technical vision
concerns: 14 years of professional experience falls one year short of the 15-year minimum requirement
No demonstrated AI/ML expertise—current role involves AI-adjacent developer tooling (MCP generators) but no evidence of building AI systems, training models, or shipping AI-powered products
Lacks Master's degree or PhD in AI-related field; background is software craftsmanship and execution rather than AI systems expertise or strategic vision

key_factors.factor: AI/ML expertise and demonstrated AI products
Director-level IC status and engineering leadership

response.justification: This is a strong software engineer with director-level experience and exceptional technical breadth, but the role is specialized for AI strategy with required 'recognized expertise in AI' and 'demonstrated products or platforms built with AI.' The candidate's AI exposure is recent (2025) and limited to developer tooling rather than AI/ML systems or AI-powered products. The 14-year experience is marginally below the 15-year baseline, and the absence of advanced AI credentials (Master's/PhD) is notable. The candidate would be excellent for a general Principal Engineer role but falls materially short of this role's specific AI strategy requirements.
